name: St. Francis Methodist School
tagline: A unique blend of Singapore-Cambridge and international curricula since 1960
description: St. Francis Methodist School (SFMS) is a private Methodist institution in Upper Bukit Timah, offering an unusually broad range of academic pathways: Singapore-Cambridge GCE O-Level, Cambridge IGCSE, Cambridge AS/A-Levels, the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme, and the Western Australian Certificate of Education (WACE). Founded in 1960 and serving approximately 700 students from over 20 countries, SFMS combines small class sizes with a values-driven Methodist ethos. The school is particularly popular among families seeking a bridging option between local and international curricula, with approximately 80% international students and a well-established track record in university placements.

admission.process: SFMS accepts applications throughout the year with monthly intakes. International students sit a placement test in English and Mathematics, followed by an interview.
requirements: School reports for the past 2 years
admission.deadlines: Rolling admissions with monthly intakes. Main academic year begins January.

faqs.question: Does SFMS offer boarding?
faqs.answer: SFMS does not operate its own boarding facilities, but the school assists international students in arranging homestay accommodation with vetted local guardians.

faqs.question: Can students transfer from the local MOE system to SFMS?
faqs.answer: Yes. SFMS is one of the few private schools that offers both Singapore-Cambridge GCE and international pathways, making it an excellent bridging option for students transitioning from the local system.

seo.title: St. Francis Methodist School Singapore — Local & International Curriculum Blend | EduAtlas
seo.description: St. Francis Methodist School in Upper Bukit Timah: Singapore-Cambridge GCE, IGCSE, IBDP and WACE pathways. Founded 1960, 700 students. View 2026 fees, admissions and programmes.
